title: Sourcing Manager IT
description:
**Hiring: Sourcing Manager IT**
 We’re looking for a
 **Sourcing Manager IT** 
 to join us at Telia.
   

  

 I’m
 **Mikael Naurin, Head of Sourcing IT** 
 , and I’m searching for someone ready to grow with us. We’re a supportive, forward-thinking team focused on
 **delivering strategic IT sourcing across software and services while managing global vendors and enabling business success.**
**What you’ll do:**
 Drive strategic IT sourcing initiatives by partnering closely with the business, applying strong collaboration and adaptability to manage vendor portfolios and support Software and Services domains across a multi-country environment.
   

  

* Develop and manage sourcing strategies across a full portfolio of global and local IT vendors
* Create value by supporting sourcing initiatives with a strategic focus on Software and Services
* Consult and collaborate closely with the business to ensure sourcing contributes to stakeholder and customer success
* Contribute to a high-performing, flexible team environment guided by Telia’s values: Dare and Care


**What will help you thrive in this role:**
 You bring strong collaboration and communication skills, thrive in a team environment, and demonstrate adaptability, proactivity, and flexibility.
   

  

* Proven experience in sourcing, preferably within IT (3–5 years)
* Strong knowledge of IT services delivery and/or software sourcing (considered an advantage)
* Ability to work collaboratively with diverse stakeholders and adapt to changing priorities
* Fluency in English


**Join us to shape smarter living for people, businesses, and the planet**
 At Telia, we’re a team of 15 000 colleagues across the Nordics and Baltics, passionate about technology and connectivity. As a
 **Leading Telecommunications Operator** 
 &
 **Climate Leader** 
 , we drive change with large-scale, impactful projects while building a sustainable, inclusive world.
   

  

 Here, you can
 **grow your skills, lead meaningful projects, and thrive in a supportive, diverse environment.** 
 Whether you’re taking your first step or your next, Telia is a place to grow, belong, and make a real impact.
   

  

**What We Offer**
 When you join us, you'll receive a comprehensive benefits package tailored to your local market, opportunities for remote work, and more. You'll also have the chance to earn a bonus based on your performance and Telia's results.
   

  

**Ready to make better happen?**
 We look forward to receiving your application by
 **June 12, 2026** 
 . Please note that we might close the position earlier if we find the right candidate, so don’t wait too long to apply!
   

  

 If you’re selected for the next stage, you’ll be contacted by our
 **Talent Acquisition Partner** 
 . Background and reference checks will be part of the process.
   

  

 Want to know more about this role? Please reach out to
 **Mikael Naurin, Head of Sourcing IT** 
 , at
 **mikael.naurin@teliacompany.com**
**Equal opportunities**
 At Telia, we recognize talent by
 **professional skills and growth potential.** 
 We champion diversity, ensuring every individual’s needs are met, regardless of disability, gender, age, nationality, sexual orientation, or social status. Together, we’re dedicated to fostering an inclusive work environment where
 **your uniqueness is our strength.**
**Join us and let’s make better happen - together!**

title: IT Project Manager to Polygon Group
description:
*Do you want to lead IT projects that make a real impact – in a global organization where technology, business and people come together?
   

  

 Polygon Group is now looking for a Project Manager to Group IT in Stockholm, who wants to ensure that IT initiatives are not only delivered, but also adopted, used correctly and create real business value.*
**Your everyday work**
 As a Project Manager in Group IT, you will be responsible for driving IT projects end‑to‑end in an international environment. The role combines IT project management with change management, with a strong focus on implementation, adoption and realization of business value.
   

  

 You will lead projects from planning to execution and follow‑up, acting as a bridge between IT, the business and external vendors. The project portfolio includes initiatives within information security, Microsoft 365 and Copilot, system implementations, improvement projects and large group‑wide programs for example within Infrastructure Solutions or Best of Breed application implementations.
   

  

 You will work closely with stakeholders across countries, prepare decision material and project documentation, and ensure that delivered solutions are not only implemented but actually used in the business. The role contributes to strengthening structure, governance and execution capability within Group IT.
   

  

**Who You Are**
 You are a structured and communicative person who enjoys working in a dynamic, evolving environment. You are comfortable taking ownership, driving initiatives forward and collaborating with many different stakeholders.
   

  

 You thrive in roles where technology, business and people intersect, and you have a strong interest in both IT and change management. You are confident presenting and communicating, and you are flexible in situations where everything is not yet fully defined.
   

  

**Requirements**
* Around 4–7 years of relevant experience, for example within IT, technology or business‑facing roles with elements of project management
* Experience of IT project management and change management
* A structured way of working and experience preparing decision support material
* High‑level understanding of IT infrastructure (cloud, workplace, security, networks)
* Strong communication skills and ability to engage stakeholders across the organization
* Experience working in an international or global environment is considered a merit
* Experience with agile delivery projects and knowledge of project models such as PMP, PRINCE2 or similar is a plus
* Fluent English, both written and spoken (Swedish is not a requirement)


**About Polygon Group**
 Global expertise. Local impact. A career that matters.
   

  

 Polygon is a global expert and the European market leader in Property Damage Control, helping to create a stronger, safer, and more sustainable society. With around 8,000 colleagues in 18 countries across three continents, we combine advanced technology, specialist knowledge, and a passion for helping people. From climate control and moisture management to emergency restoration after unexpected incidents or natural disasters, we deliver tailored solutions that make a real difference. Join us and be part of a team that safeguards property and supports communities - always by their side when it matters most.
   

  

 We are based in modern offices on Mäster Samuelsgatan in central Stockholm.
   

  

**Contact**
 In this recruitment, Polygon Group is collaborating with Wise IT. If you would like to know more, please contact the responsible Recruitment Consultant Sandra Åhs at Sandra.ahs@wise.se
   

  

 Unfortunately, we cannot accept applications via email as we are committed to protecting your personal privacy. You can read more about how we process personal data in accordance with GDPR in Wise Group’s Data Processing Policy.
   

  

 We review applications on an ongoing basis, including the selection of candidates who will be invited to interview.
   

  

 We look forward to receiving your application!

title: Senior IT Security Consultant
description:
We are looking for a  Senior IT Security Consultant to join us at Telia Cygate.
   

  

 My name is Lukas Carlsson, and I have the privilege of leading our amazing security consulting team in the Eastern Region. We’re a team that truly enjoys what we do — we have fun at work while continuously learning, challenging ourselves, and delivering top-class solutions our clients can rely on.
   

  

 Right now, we’re growing and looking for curious, driven IT Security Consultants who want to be part of something exciting. With us, you won’t just sharpen your technical skills — you’ll join a team where
 **collaboration, laughter, and personal growth** 
 are just as important as technical excellence.
   

  

 Want to enjoy your work, grow every day, and make a real impact?
   

  

 Then we should talk!
   

  

**What you’ll do:**
 As an IT Security Consultant with us, you will work closely with your colleagues in a wide range of client projects. You’ll be involved in everything from hands-on implementation projects—where you dive deep into installation, design, migration, configuration, and troubleshooting of cutting-edge security solutions—to taking on a more advisory role, guiding customers through today’s complex security landscape.
   

  

 Our clients trust us to secure their IT environments. We don’t just help them run and maintain their systems — we also support them in planning ahead and future-proofing their solutions.
   

  

 With us, you won’t work in a silo. Our projects often span multiple service areas, meaning you’ll collaborate with experts in networking, security, and Hybrid IT. This gives you the opportunity to build both
 **deep technical expertise and broad competence** 
 , covering everything from network and security operations to cloud, automation, virtualization, and data centers.
   

  

 And if you’re eager to get certified in new areas? We’ll support you every step of the way.
   

  

**What will help you thrive in this role:** 
  

  

 We’re looking for someone who is passionate about solving customer challenges and enjoys engaging with both technical specialists and decision-makers, presenting solutions in a clear and inspiring way.
   

  

 You’re comfortable in an advisory role and motivated by helping customers find the right solutions for their needs. You have solid experience working with security solutions, with a focus on one or more product areas.
   

  

* Experience working with solutions from Checkpoint, Fortinet, Infoblox, and F5.
* Strong background in IT security and networking, with a focus on firewalls, DNS/DHCP, and load balancing.
* Deep technical understanding of network protocols and infrastructure services.
* Comfortable working in complex environments with high requirements for security, availability, and performance.
* Experience with design, implementation, and troubleshooting of security solutions in both on-premises and hybrid cloud environments.
* Swedish native speaker and fluency in English, both spoken and written.


**Why Telia Cygate?** 
  

  

 At Telia Cygate, we’re not only experts in networks, security, and various types of cloud solutions – we’re also a team that believes work should be fun! As part of Telia Company, we have the strength to create real impact on a large scale, contribute to societal value, and help our customers navigate the world of digitalization. And we always do it with warmth, care, and a smile. Here, it’s our ideas and diverse perspectives that make us strong, and together we ensure that the world of technology becomes a place where everyone feels good – and where you belong.
   

  

 Your development matters to us. We encourage continuous learning and make sure that you and your interests are in focus. We know that people are the heart of our company, which is why we value joy, creativity, and different perspectives in everything we do.
   

  

 Welcome to grow together with us – where we make technology human and create real impact!
   

  

**Telia Cygate** 

**is proud to be certificated as** 

**Great** 

**Place to** 

**Work!** 
  

  

 With us, people are at the center – here you get the chance to grow, be challenged, and thrive together with dedicated colleagues. We are building a culture where security, togetherness, and opportunities go hand in hand. Do you want to be part of the journey? Welcome to a workplace that truly lives up to its name.
   

  

**What we offer** 
  

  

 When you join us, you'll receive a comprehensive benefits package tailored to your local market, opportunities for remote work, and more. You'll also have the chance to earn a bonus based on your performance and Telia's results.
   

  

**Interested? Apply!**
 We look forward to receiving your application by
 **2026-06-22.** 

 Please note that we might close the position earlier if we find the right candidate, so don’t wait too long to apply!
   

  

 Location: Solna
   

  

 If you’re selected for the next stage, you’ll be contacted by our
 **Talent Acquisition Partner.** 

 Background and reference checks will be part of the process.
   

  

 Want to know more about this role? Please reach out to Lukas Carlsson at lukas.carlsson@cygate.se
   

  

 We kindly ask recruitment agencies and external vendors not to contact us regarding this position.
   

  

**Equal opportunities**
 At Telia, we recognize talent by professional skills and growth potential. We champion diversity, ensuring every individual’s needs are met, regardless of disability, gender, age, nationality, sexual orientation, or social status. Together, we’re dedicated to fostering an inclusive work environment where your uniqueness is our strength.
   

  

**Welcome to Telia** 

**Cygate** 

**– Home of** 

**Experts**

title: Data Engineer
description:
*Kreditz is transforming how businesses assess credit and risk using real-time data and NextGen Credit Scoring solutions.*
*As one of Europe’s fastest-growing FinTech scaleups - we’ve earned a place on the* 
***Sifted 100: Nordics & Benelux***
*list in both 2024 and 2025 and most recently, we were proud to receive a* 
***double win at the Credit Management & Compliance Awards 2025***
*, being named both "* 
***Fintech Rocket of the Year***
*" and "* 
***Breakthrough of the Year***
*".*
*With strong growth across the Nordics and Europe, our clients range from leading banks and iGaming giants to fast-moving digital innovators.*
**About The Position**
*At Kreditz, data is not just a support function, it is the core of our product. We turn raw bank transactions into real-time insight that powers credit & risk decisions across Europe.*
*As we scale, we’re strengthening our data foundation and building a modern, reliable, and cost-efficient data platform. We’re now looking for a Data Engineer to join our Product team and take ownership of our Data Warehouse, pipelines, and reporting enablement across the business.*
*This is a hands-on role for someone who loves building scalable data infrastructure and wants to work close to both product and real business impact.*
*Your mission is to build and evolve a scalable, reliable, and high-performing data environment that supports both analytics and operational needs.*
**What Can Kreditz Offer**
* A once-in-a-lifetime growth journey, from startup to Global scaleup
* A journey where everyone in the team plays a crucial role for our company’s success
* Fun-loving, driven, and passionate colleagues who support each other’s success
* A culture that celebrates progress, humility, and teamwork
* We win together
* 24/7 access to gym, sauna and sports facilities at our office, plus additional wellness allowance
* Competitive base salary


**RESPONSIBILITES**
**Some Of The Main Responsibilities You Will Cover**
* Maintain & Enhance the DWH: Own the architecture of our Data Warehouse, ensuring it is scalable, cost-effective, and optimized for both analytical and operational needs.
* Pipeline Automation: Design, build, and maintain robust ETL/ELT pipelines to ingest data from various sources into our AWS ecosystem. Work closely with our MLOPs Engineering team to assist the data pipelines for our AI / ML models
* Stakeholder Enablement: Work closely with Product, Finance, and Commercial teams to automate internal reporting and create self-service data environments.
* AWS Infrastructure: Leverage and manage AWS services (such as Glue, Redshift, Athena, Lambda, and S3) to ensure a high-performing data environment.
* Data Quality & Governance: Implement monitoring and validation frameworks to ensure data integrity and lineage across all systems.
* Collaborate: Partner with our DevOps and Backend teams to align infrastructure and ensure smooth data flow across the entire Kreditz platform.


**WE ARE LOOKING FOR**
 We focus on practical engineering skill, strong foundations, and the ability to build systems that last.
   

  

**In Addition To That We Believe You Have**
* BSc / MSc in a quantitative subject, i.e., Physics, Engineering, Mathematics, Computer Science or equivalent.
* 3+ years of experience in Data Engineering or a similar role focused on data infrastructure.
* Expert-level SQL skills for complex transformations and performance tuning.
* Proficiency in Python for building pipelines and automating tasks.
* Strong hands-on experience with AWS (specifically services like Redshift/Snowflake, S3, Glue, and Lambda).
* Solid understanding of data warehousing concepts (e.g., Star Schema, Kimball) and modern data stack tools.
* Experience with orchestration tools (e.g., Airflow, Dagster) and CI/CD workflows (i.e., GitHub Actions).
* Ability to translate complex technical data structures into clear insights for non-technical stakeholders.


**Meritorious Skills**
* Experience with Infrastructure as Code (Terraform).
* Experience from FinTech, Open Banking, or working with highly regulated data.
* Familiarity with BI tools (e.g., AWS QuickSight, PowerBI...etc)


**WHY JOIN NOW?**
 At Kreditz, you’ll work with data that matters, powering real-time financial decisions across Europe. You’ll have real ownership, real impact, and the opportunity to shape the data foundation of a fast-growing FinTech scaleup.
   

  

**APPOINTMENT OF ROLE**
 Q2/Q3 2026
   

  

**SCOPE**
 Full-time, permanent employment (tillsvidareanställning)
   

  

**SALARY**
 Competitive salary
   

  

**REPORTING**
 You will be reporting to the company’s CPO
   

  

**WORKPLACE**
 Danderyd, Stockholm
   

  

**RECRUITMENT PROCESS**
 Ongoing selection, we review candidates continuously.
   

  

*Last day for applications 31st March*
**CRIMINAL RECORDS EXTRACT**
 As part of our recruitment process we will ask you to present a clean criminal records extract
   

  

**CONTACT**
 Lorenzo Puccio, VP Strategic Operations, Lorenzo@kreditz.com
   

  

 For more information about the role feel free to reach out to our CPO, Spilios Tzouras, spilios.tzouras@kreditz.com
   

  

 Intelligent credit- & risk decisions made easy with open banking & AI-technology

title: Channel Manager
description:
IFS is a billion-dollar revenue company with 7000+ employees on all continents. Our leading AI technology is the backbone of our award-winning enterprise software solutions, enabling our customers to be their best when it really matters–at the Moment of Service™. Our commitment to internal AI adoption has allowed us to stay at the forefront of technological advancements, ensuring our colleagues can unlock their creativity and productivity, and our solutions are always cutting-edge.
   

  





 At IFS, we’re flexible, we’re innovative, and we’re focused not only on how we can engage with our customers but on how we can make a real change and have a worldwide impact. We help solve some of society’s greatest challenges, fostering a better future through our agility, collaboration, and trust.
   

  





 We celebrate diversity and understand our responsibility to reflect the diverse world we work in. We are committed to promoting an inclusive workforce that fully represents the many different cultures, backgrounds, and viewpoints of our customers, our partners, and our communities. As a truly international company serving people from around the globe, we realize that our success is tantamount to the respect we have for those different points of view.
   

  





 By joining our team, you will have the opportunity to be part of a
 **global, diverse environment;** 
 you will be joining a
 **winning team** 
 with a
 **commitment to sustainability;**
 and a company where we get things done so that you can
 **make a positive impact** 
 on the world.
   

  





 We’re looking for innovative and original thinkers to work in an environment where you can
 **#MakeYourMoment** 
 so that we can help others make theirs. With the power of our AI-driven solutions, we empower our team to change the status quo and make a real difference.
   

  





 If you want to change the status quo, we’ll help you make your moment. Join Team Purple. Join IFS.
   

  

  

  




**Job Description**

 We are seeking an experienced Channel Manager to build, develop, and scale a high-performing partner ecosystem that drives sustainable business growth. In this role, you will own the end-to-end partner lifecycle, from recruitment and onboarding through to capability development and pipeline generation.
   

  





 You will work closely with strategic partners to create long-term growth plans, strengthen go-to-market execution, and build self-sufficient partner capabilities across sales, presales, marketing, and delivery functions. This is a strategic role focused on enabling partner success and creating scalable revenue opportunities through a thriving ecosystem.
   

  





 Key Responsibilities
   

  





 Partner Relationship Management
   

  




* Act as the primary point of contact for assigned partners.
* Build and maintain strong relationships with partner leadership and operational teams.
* Develop and manage joint business plans with clear growth objectives and performance targets.
* Monitor and review partner performance across pipeline, capability development, and business growth.



 Partner Recruitment & Go-to-Market Strategy
   

  




* Identify, recruit, and onboard new partners aligned with business priorities and market opportunities.
* Develop and execute partner go-to-market strategies.
* Align partner positioning with target industries, customer segments, and value propositions.
* Ensure successful integration of new partners into the ecosystem.



 Partner Enablement & Capability Development
   

  




* Drive partner capability development across sales, presales, marketing, and delivery.
* Support certification, training, and skills development initiatives.
* Assess partner maturity and implement structured development plans.
* Enable partners to become increasingly self-sufficient in generating and delivering business.



 Marketing & Demand Generation
   

  




* Collaborate with marketing teams and partners on joint go-to-market initiatives.
* Support the development of demand generation campaigns and pipeline creation programs.
* Ensure partner participation in marketing activities that generate qualified opportunities.



 Pipeline Development
   

  




* Drive the creation of partner-sourced pipeline opportunities.
* Monitor pipeline health, quality, and coverage across the partner portfolio.
* Ensure partners consistently identify and develop new business opportunities.
* Transition qualified opportunities to the Channel Sales Manager for progression through the sales cycle.



 Ecosystem Growth & Governance
   

  




* Support partner progression through programme tiers and maturity levels.
* Establish regular business reviews, KPIs, and performance governance.
* Ensure compliance with partner programme requirements and engagement models.
* Provide ecosystem performance insights and recommendations to leadership.



  

  

  

  




**Qualifications**

 Essential
   

  




* Proven experience in channel management, partner management, alliance management, or ecosystem development.
* Demonstrated success in building and growing strategic partner relationships.
* Experience developing and executing partner go-to-market strategies and business plans.
* Strong understanding of sales, presales, marketing, and service delivery models.
* Excellent stakeholder management, relationship-building, and communication skills.
* Ability to operate strategically while driving structured execution and measurable outcomes.



 Preferred
   

  




* Experience working within enterprise software, SaaS, technology, or consulting environments.
* Experience managing partner programmes across multiple regions or industries.
* Knowledge of partner enablement frameworks and channel growth strategies.



 Success Measures
   

  





 Success in this role will be measured by:
   

  




* Growth in partner-generated pipeline.
* Increased partner capability, maturity, and self-sufficiency.
* Expansion of partner ecosystem coverage across industries, geographies, and competencies.
* Progression of partners through programme tiers.
* Overall health, performance, and sustainability of the partner ecosystem.


**Additional Information**

*We embrace flexibility and hybrid work opportunities to support diverse needs and lifestyles, while also valuing inclusive workplace experiences. By fostering a sense of community, we drive innovation, strengthen connections, and nurture belonging. Our commitment ensures you can work in a way that suits you best, while also engaging with colleagues to share ideas and build meaningful relationships.*

title: Facilities Manager, Data Center Operations
description:
**Minimum qualifications:**
* Bachelor's degree in a technical field or equivalent practical experience.
* 8 years of experience in the management of teams performing installation, maintenance, or upgrade work in data center or business-critical facilities.
* 7 years of experience in heating, ventilating, and air conditioning (HVAC), electrical, controls, water chemistry, generators, or other similar technical domains.
* 5 years of experience in data centers, hospitals, hotel industry, power plants, High Voltage (HV) electrical substations, or other mission-critical facility maintenance and operations.


**Preferred qualifications:**
* Experience managing a mission-critical facilities portfolio or operations.
* Understanding of the operations and maintenance of electrical, mechanical, and control systems in a data center environment.
* Ability to develop and grow team members, through coaching, mentoring, and training.
* Excellent people management and leadership skills, with the ability to influence at all levels.


**About the job** 
 The Data Center team designs and operates some of the most sophisticated electrical engineering, mechanical engineering and Heating, Ventilation and Air Conditioning (HVAC) systems in the world. Facility Technicians at Google data centers operate, monitor and support physical facilities conditions. Some duties will include heating and cooling of air and water, power supply, generators, UPS systems, electrical distribution and control and monitoring systems. You regularly help inspect, maintain and repair various data center systems such as piping and non-critical electrical or mechanical system components). You'll provide daily assistance to senior technicians as you read blueprints/schematics, conduct tours of systems and assess their working order.
   

  

 You will develop creative approaches to reducing operational costs while improving overall data center efficiency. You ensure that environmental and safety standards are consistently met, identifying problems and making repairs quickly In emergency situations or abnormal conditions, you manage data center performance issues and outages to minimize the recovery time from failures. Behind everything our users see online is the architecture built by the Technical Infrastructure team to keep it running. From developing and maintaining our data centers to building the next-generation of Google platforms, we make Google's product portfolio possible. We're proud to be our engineers' engineers and love voiding warranties by taking things apart so we can rebuild them. We keep our networks up and running, ensuring our users have the best and fastest experience possible.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Empower your team through hiring, talent development, and career support, while integrating Google-wide strategy with internal and external partners, ensuring aligned goals.
* Direct operational budgets and expense forecasting, formulate and execute cost-effective strategies with a focus on total cost of ownership.
* Fulfill service level agreements (SLAs) for uptime, efficiency, and cost with internal teams, set strategy and plan deployments across multiple sites.
* Apply critical thinking and innovation in addressing traditional and emergent data center issues, while demonstrating flexibility and the ability to support operations, taking the lead in responding to critical events when necessary.
* Implement and drive a safety culture at the site, while leading by example, deliver results, develop people, and build community.


 Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

title: SAP Data Engineer
description:
**For a Sustainable Future Together**
 Coop is Sweden's largest cooperative and a leading player in the grocery retail sector, with over 4 million members. We focus heavily on sustainability and work to create a greener and more just future by offering sustainable products and services.
   

  

 We believe that a diversity of perspectives and experiences is key to creating a more inclusive and successful workplace. Therefore, we welcome everyone, regardless of gender, age, background, or ethnic origin.
   

  

 Coop Sverige – Together we make food-Sweden sustainable
   

  

 At Coop we are building the data backbone of a truly data-driven company. We run stores for our 4.2 million members, and our motto is “Relevance in every touchpoint.” Understanding our customers’ needs and behaviours is what fuels everything we do – from purchasing and logistics to personalised customer experiences.
   

  

 We are now strengthening our data organisation with an SAP Data Engineer who will help connect our strong SAP estate to the next generation of AI and analytics at Coop.
   

  

**About The Role**
 You will be part of our Data Platform team, which sits within Coop’s AI & Business Intelligence organisation – the team building the data foundation that powers analytics and AI across the company.
   

  

 This is a role at the intersection of two worlds. You will build and manage data models, transformations and integrations in SAP Datasphere and SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) – and, just as importantly, make that SAP data flow into CAP, our Azure-based data platform, where it becomes available to data scientists, analysts and business users across the company.
   

  

 In other words: you don’t just build inside SAP. You make SAP data usable everywhere. You will be central to integrating our SAP S/4HANA and analytical data into CAP, so that high-quality, well-modelled data powers AI/ML, self-service analytics and better decisions throughout Coop.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
* Develop data models, transformations and integrations in SAP Datasphere and SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC).
* Integrate and deliver SAP data into CAP, our Azure data platform, ensuring it is reliable, well-structured and ready for downstream analytics and AI/ML use.
* Refine data from SAP systems – ideally S/4HANA – into products and insights that drive real business value.
* Design and build engaging reports and dashboards in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) that make data accessible and easy to understand.
* Safeguard data quality, performance and usability across the full data flow.
* Partner with the business to turn requirements into robust, scalable technical solutions, and contribute across the whole development lifecycle – testing, troubleshooting and deployment included.


***Who are you?***
 You are someone who is energised by turning data into insight and opportunity. You see the potential in building something new – and you want to be part of it from the start. You enjoy close collaboration, sharing knowledge and solving problems together, and you move naturally between thinking strategically and rolling up your sleeves to deliver.
   

  

**We believe that you**
* Have experience developing and optimising data solutions in SAP Datasphere, SAP HANA or SAP BW.
* Have a solid command of SQL, data integration and data modelling, with an eye for quality and performance in data flows.
* Understand ETL processes and how to extract, transform and load data efficiently and reliably.
* Know CDS (Core Data Services) and how CDS views are designed for flexible, high-performance models.
* Are comfortable refining data from SAP source systems (ideally S/4HANA) into insights that support the business.
* Have experience building reports and dashboards in SAP Analytics Cloud.
* Can communicate and collaborate effectively in cross-functional teams, discussing solutions with both technical specialists and non-technical stakeholders.


**It’s meritorious if you**
* Have hands-on experience with SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC).
* Have worked with cloud data platforms – preferably Azure (e.g. Databricks, data lake / medallion architecture) and understand how SAP and cloud estates connect.
* Have familiarity with core SAP S/4 business processes (sales, purchasing, inventory, finance).
* Have worked with DevOps and agile delivery.


**As a Person, You Are**
* Engaged and humble – you help out where it’s needed.
* Curious and solution-oriented – you see challenges as opportunities.
* Structured and thorough – you build for long-term sustainability.
* Communicative and collaborative – you create clarity and shared understanding.


**About Us**
 The grocery trade is a stable, long-term industry, and Coop’s offering is essential to the transition toward a greener, more sustainable future. You’ll join a culture defined by humility, initiative and strong values, where we take joint responsibility and genuinely want to help each other succeed. Our office in Solna Business Park offers a modern work environment with great transport links, a fantastic staff restaurant, and the option of hybrid work in agreement with your manager.
   

  

 If you have questions about the position, you are welcome to contact Hiring Manager Jakob Liljedahl (jakob.liljedahl@coop.se) or the recruiter Andreas Nilsson (Andreas.nilsson2@coop.se).
   

  

 Welcome with your application!

title: PR & Marketing Intern
description:
**About Transparent** 




 We're a passionate team dedicated to designing high-quality, meaningful, and beautiful speakers that seamlessly blend into any space. We believe in the power of meticulous design – every detail, from the perfect typographic balance to considered colour palettes. Transparent is more than just a word - it's a commitment to authenticity and seeing the world for what it is. Since our founding in Stockholm in 2012, we've been an independent brand challenging the unsustainable norms of the tech industry. We're looking for a like-minded PR & Marketing Intern to join our team and help craft the future of the Transparent brand.
 



  





**Role Overview** 




 As our PR & Marketing Intern, you'll work closely with our founders, and play a key role in refining our global social presence. You'll be responsible for supporting with copywriting, Gifting activities, social media curation and everything in between. As we are a small and dynamic team you will also be asked to help out in other areas where it makes sense. We seek passionate, ambitious, smart, and, above all, kind individuals. We welcome those from diverse backgrounds with different perspectives on problem-solving.
 



  





**What You'll Do:** 




 ●
 **Social Media Coordination** 
 : Support the planning, production, and scheduling of engaging content across social platforms, including Instagram, TikTok, Pinterest, Youtube, and paid media channels
 



 ●
 **PR & Gifting** 
 : Supporting incoming gifting requests, aswell as supporting reachouts
 



 ●
 **Copywriting** 
 : Help develop our tone of voice on our social media platforms that reflect the brand’s tone and visual identity
 



  





**Who You Are:** 




 ● A creative thinker with a discerning eye for design and an understanding of the social media landscape (TikTok, Instagram, Pinterest, YouTube)
 



 ● Currently pursuing or recently completed a degree in Marketing, Communications, Fashion, Media, Public Relations, or a related field
 



 ● Results-oriented with the ability to meet deadlines
 



 ● Willing to learn and grow in a dynamic and fast-moving environment
 



 ● Reliable and meticulous in your work
 



  





**What We Offer:** 




 ●
 **Real-World Experience** 
 : Gain hands-on experience working closely with the founders of a growing company that celebrates meticulous, detail-oriented design work.
 



 ●
 **Mentorship and Growth** 
 : Learn the ins and outs of building a successful, progressive and value driven brand identity
 



 ●
 **Dynamic and global environment** 
 : Transparent work on many exciting collaborative projects across the world with some of the biggest names in music and the creative industry.
 



 ●
 **Creative Impact** 
 : See your ideas and designs come to life and have a direct impact on a brand that values creativity.
 



  





**Start** 
 : September - January 2026
 



**Location:** 
 Södermalm, Sweden
 



  





 This is an unpaid position with potential growing into a part or full-time position upon successful completion of the 6 month internship.

title: Business Analyst sökes – Betalningar & Datatransformation
description:
Vill du vara med och driva transformation inom betalningar och data i en komplex bankmiljö?
   

  

 Vi söker nu en
 **Senior Business Analyst** 
 till ett omfattande transformationsarbete inom
 **betalningsområdet** 
 , där du får en central roll i att samla in, analysera och omsätta verksamhetsbehov till strukturerade krav och lösningar.
   

  

 Det här uppdraget passar dig som är van att arbeta i komplexa IT-miljöer, har lång erfarenhet från banksektorn och trivs i gränslandet mellan verksamhet, teknik och förändringsarbete.
   

  

**Om rollen**
 Som Business Analyst kommer du att spela en viktig roll i ett större transformationsinitiativ kopplat till
 **payments och data** 
 . Rollen innebär nära samarbete med flera team och intressenter där fokus ligger på att driva kravarbete, koordinera refinement-processer och säkerställa att verksamhetsbehov översätts till tydliga funktionella och tekniska krav.
   

  

 Du kommer att arbeta i en agil miljö och vara involverad i flera parallella initiativ där struktur, kommunikation och analytisk förmåga blir avgörande för framgång.
   

  

**Arbetsuppgifter**
 I rollen kommer du bland annat att:
   

  

* Leda, koordinera och driva refinementarbete tillsammans med verksamhet och utvecklingsteam.
* Samla in, analysera och dokumentera verksamhetskrav samt funktionella och systemrelaterade kravspecifikationer.
* Arbeta nära stakeholders på flera nivåer inom organisationen, exempelvis:
+ verksamhetsexperter
+ produktledning
+ arkitekter
+ utvecklingsteam
+ QA/testteam
+ ämnesexperter (SME)

* Säkerställa tydlig scope och kravbild för olika initiativ och förändringar.
* Facilitera workshops och samla in krav direkt från verksamheten.
* Hantera flera parallella initiativ och bidra till koordinering mellan olika projekt och team.
* Arbeta strukturerat med kravdokumentation i komplexa systemlandskap.


**Vi söker dig som har**
 För att lyckas i rollen ser vi att du uppfyller följande krav:
   

  

**Obligatoriska krav**
* Minst 15 års erfarenhet som Business Analyst inom IT.
* Dokumenterad erfarenhet av arbete i agila utvecklingsmiljöer.
* Erfarenhet från bank- och finanssektorn.
* Tidigare erfarenhet inom betalningsområdet (Payments).
* Teknisk bakgrund eller teknisk utbildning.
* Tidigare erfarenhet av arbete som utvecklare eller testare.
* Erfarenhet av att arbeta i komplexa systemmiljöer och större förändringsinitiativ.
* Van att arbeta med flera parallella projekt och initiativ samtidigt.
* Dokumenterad erfarenhet av kravinsamling direkt från stakeholders, exempelvis genom workshops.
* Mycket god förmåga att uttrycka dig på svenska och engelska, både i tal och skrift.


**Personliga egenskaper**
 Vi tror att du är:
   

  

* Analytisk och strukturerad med stor noggrannhet i detaljer.
* Kommunikativ och trygg i dialog med olika typer av stakeholders.
* Självgående och initiativtagande även i otydliga situationer.
* Lösningsorienterad, pragmatisk och anpassningsbar.
* Van att navigera i komplexa organisationer och förändringsmiljöer.
* Prestigelös med stark samarbetsförmåga.


**Omfattning och placering**
* Omfattning: 100 %
* Plats: Stockholm, Arenastaden
* Distansarbete: Ej aktuellt
* Uppdragsperiod: augusti 2026 – juni 2027


 Det här är en möjlighet för dig som vill bidra i ett strategiskt transformationsarbete inom betalningar och arbeta i en affärskritisk miljö där kravhantering och samverkan är avgörande för framgång.

title: IT-Project Manager to Deloitte’s internal Nordic IT organization
description:
Deloitte is a global leader in Audit & Assurance, Tax & Legal and Consultative Services. With more than 470,000 employees in over 150 countries, we help our clients and our communities solve complex problems and achieve ambitious goals through domain and industry expertise, global reach and alliances with the largest tech providers. What defines Deloitte is our drive to make a meaningful difference. For us, this means long-term development and results for our clients and their companies, ideas and actions that also contribute to positive societal development. It is also about empowering our people and promoting their development.
 



  





**Job Description** 




 We are now looking for an experienced IT-Project Manager to join our internal Nordic IT organization. As IT Project Manager, you will lead the end-to-end delivery of Nordic IT projects – from initiation to closure – working closely with colleagues across the Nordic organization. This is a hands-on role where you combine structured project management with real accountability for scope, schedule, budget and value delivery.
 



  





 The role is Nordic in scope, with English as the primary working language. You will report to the Head of Portfolio & Projects and work in a matrix organization without direct staff responsibility, but with significant leadership impact.
 



  





 The gross monthly salary for this position ranges from 40,000 to 50,000 SEK.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities** 



* **Drive project delivery**



 Take ownership of assigned Nordic IT projects and lead them day-to-day. Develop and maintain structured project plans, define milestones and deliverables, follow up on progress and coordinate cross-country and cross-functional teams to ensure integrated execution.
 



  




* **Collaborate with Project Owner and SteerCo**



 Work closely with the Project Owner and Steering Committee to align scope, priorities and decisions. Prepare clear status updates and decision material for the IT Portfolio Management Board and other governance forums, ensuring your projects follow our Nordic IT governance framework and ways of working.
 



  




* **Manage risks and dependencies**



 Proactively identify, assess and mitigate project risks and issues. Understand and manage dependencies across projects and service domains, help avoid bottlenecks and drive structured issue resolution together with relevant stakeholders.
 



  




* **Collaborate with stakeholders and vendors**



 Engage IT teams, business stakeholders and external vendors to secure alignment on objectives, scope and expectations. Build strong relationships and maintain open, transparent and to-the-point communication on progress, risks and impacts.
 



  




* **Follow up on value and progress**



 Define and communicate expected project benefits together with the Project Owner and portfolio team. Track progress against scope, time, cost and quality, support benefit tracking and contribute to post-implementation reviews to ensure that the project delivers real, measurable value.
 



  




* **Manage resources and budget**



 Plan and manage project resources and budget within approved limits. Provide accurate forecasts and status reporting, and work with the IT Business Controller and other stakeholders to track costs, validate funding and adjust plans when needed.
 



  





**Qualifications** 




**We are looking for candidates who have:** 



* Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field
* 2–5 years of experience leading IT projects in complex, matrixed organizations, preferably in a Nordic or international context
* Proven expertise in project planning and execution using Waterfall, Hybrid and/or Agile methodologies
* Strong experience from driving complex, multi-stakeholder and multi-country IT initiatives
* Excellent skills in risk identification, assessment and mitigation
* Solid understanding of governance frameworks and structured reporting
* Strong communication, facilitation, negotiation and influencing skills – able to turn strategies into concrete actions
* Experience working independently and driving projects forward with high maturity and accountability
* Comfortable collaborating across borders and cultures, and managing different working styles in the Nordic region
* English on a professional level; Swedish is a plus
* Valid work permit in Sweden is required



  





**Additional Information** 




**What we offer** 




  





 As part of Deloitte, you gain access to the world's largest consulting network with colleagues around the globe that you can leverage to develop yourself, our clients, and our business. You will work on exciting, complex projects at both local and Nordic levels, in a team that always values your perspective and is at the forefront of digitalization.
 



  





 We place great emphasis on offering you opportunities to drive your personal development through practical experience, a personal coach who supports you in your career, and continuous feedback from experienced colleagues. You have the opportunity to work in a company that help our clients on their journey toward a more sustainable organization, while we work purposefully toward our own ambitions in climate, environment, and social responsibility. We see value in our employees having diverse experiences, which promotes innovation and contributes to multiple perspectives.
 



  





**How to Apply:** 




 You apply online. Your application should consist of:
 


* CV
* A short motivation for why you are interested in the role



  





**Please submit your application as soon as possible, as we work with ongoing selection.** 




  





 At Deloitte, we value different skills, perspectives, and experiences, as it enables us to effectively solve complex challenges for our clients. We encourage you to apply for the position if you believe your skills can contribute to our team´s success.
 



  





*As a qualitative part of Deloitte's recruitment process, our final candidates undergo a background check, to increase awareness of our future employees, secure the working environment for our employees and meet regulatory requirements. The background check process is carried out in collaboration with ToFindOut. https://tofindout.se/en/background-check/* 




  





**Questions?** 




 If you have any questions regarding the role or the application process, please contact Viktor Lundeberg Bornebusch, Manager Talent Acquisition, at vbornebusch@deloitte.se.
 



  





**We are looking forward to your application!** 




  





**What impact will you make?** 




 Every day, your work will make an impact that matters, while you thrive in a dynamic culture of inclusion, collaboration and high performance. We do not just aspire to make solutions. We wish to create an impact that makes a positive and crucial difference for the people around us, our clients and our society. Deloitte is where you’ll find unlimited opportunities to succeed and realize your full potential.

title: Inbound Sales Representative
description:
**Inbound Sales Representative at atNorth**
 atNorth is a leading Nordic data center company providing cost-effective, scalable high-density colocation and built-to-suit services to industry-leading organizations across Iceland, Sweden, Finland, and Denmark.
   

  

 With sustainability at our core, our data centers run on renewable energy and are designed according to circular economy principles. Through innovative design, power efficiency, and intelligent operations, we deliver reliable, secure, and high-performance infrastructure.
   

  

 Headquartered in Reykjavik, Iceland, atNorth operates eight data centers across the Nordics, with additional sites under development in Finland, Denmark, and Sweden.
   

  

 We combine world-class technology with a people-first culture built on collaboration, ownership, and trust, empowering our teams to grow, take responsibility, and make a real impact.
   

  

 By joining our international team, you will help shape the future of sustainable digital infrastructure.
   

  

**Inbound Sales Representative at atNorth**
 This role is positioned at the center of our inbound commercial flow, acting as the first point of contact for new opportunities and playing a key role in ensuring a structured, efficient, and scalable sales process. You will enable the broader Sales organization to focus on larger and more complex deals, while independently managing smaller, standardized opportunities.
   

  

 You will be responsible for qualifying inbound leads and leading sales opportunities up to a defined size, using standardized solutions, pricing, and agreements. The role requires a combination of commercial understanding, structured execution, and strong communication skills.
   

  

**Key responsibilities:**
 A key focus of the role is to ensure a fast, consistent, and high-quality handling of inbound opportunities. You will assess and qualify leads, manage standardized proposal processes, and ensure efficient handover across the organization.
   

  

 You will be responsible for initial qualification of all inbound opportunities in accordance with atNorth’s qualification framework and dispatch relevant opportunities to the appropriate Sales representatives based on customer segment and scope.
   

  

 You will own the full proposal process for smaller colocation opportunities, utilizing predefined templates, solutions, and pricing models to ensure efficiency and quick turnaround times. This includes managing client communication throughout the bid process, clarifying requirements, presenting solutions, and addressing any questions.
   

  

 From a commercial and contractual perspective, you will adapt standard colocation agreements within predefined thresholds, ensuring alignment with atNorth’s pricing policies and deal structures.
   

  

 Beyond execution, you will ensure proper documentation of all proposals and customer interactions, manage contract closure, and drive the formal handover to Operations and Finance.
   

  

 You will also contribute to continuous improvement of the standardized bid process and support outbound initiatives, including targeted outreach and preparation of sales materials in collaboration with the high-density colocation team.
   

  

**What We Are Looking For**
 We are looking for someone who thrives in a structured sales environment and enjoys working with standardized solutions while maintaining a strong customer focus.
   

  

**Your profile**
* Bachelor’s degree in Business, Engineering, Computer Science, or similar
* Minimum 2 years of experience working with customer contracts within IT, infrastructure, or real estate
* Experience with digital sales, inbound handling, or standardized solution selling is an advantage
* Strong digital understanding, including experience with IT systems, platforms, and AI tools
* Professional proficiency in English, both written and spoken


 You are detail-oriented and committed to delivering high-quality outputs. You are a quick learner with a strong ability to work with new systems and tools, and you bring a service-minded and responsive approach to your work. You are able to work under pressure, manage multiple tasks, and meet tight deadlines while maintaining a strong commercial mindset.
   

  

**Decision-making & impact**
 You will have responsibility for managing and closing standardized proposal processes within defined commercial frameworks and authority limits. You will operate in accordance with atNorth’s Bid Process, Sales Strategy, and Delegation of Authority, while actively contributing to improving how we manage and scale smaller colocation opportunities.
   

  

**About The AtNorth Culture**
 We believe that sustainable success starts with the wellbeing of our people. For us, an open, transparent, and inclusive culture is more than a priority, it is the core of how we lead and innovate.
   

  

 We don’t just talk about culture; we nurture it. Through regular one-to-one engagements and monthly wellbeing surveys, we listen carefully to our teams, ensuring that every voice is heard and every opportunity for improvement is embraced.
   

  

 We recognize that innovation and collaboration thrive where people feel truly connected, which is why we come together each week to share ideas, strengthen relationships, and nurture a sense of belonging.
   

  

 At atNorth, we are building a community where you can do your best work and shape a sustainable future together.

title: Category Manager IT
description:
Are you passionate about shaping IT procurement strategies and driving value in a global, matrix organization? ASSA ABLOY Global Solutions is looking for an experienced and strategic Category Manager IT to join our team.
   

  

 In this key role, you will lead procurement activities with a primary focus on the IT category, while also potentially supporting other indirect categories. You will work closely with senior stakeholders, including CIO/CTO organizations, to drive cost efficiency, ensure compliant IT contracts aligned with legal, security, and data privacy requirements and strengthen governance across the division.
   

  

 In this role, you'll thrive in a collaborative, innovative culture that empowers you to build a career to be proud of.
   

  

**What You Will Do As Our Category Manager IT**
 As our Category Manager IT, you will take ownership of defining and implementing category strategies for IT procurement, ensuring we select the right suppliers, optimize spend, and create long-term value for the business.
   

  

 You will play a key role in strengthening procurement governance, driving cross-divisional alignment, and ensuring transparency across IT spend and performance.
   

  

 You would also:
   

  

* Define and implement IT category strategies to optimize spend, streamline the supplier base, and align procurement efforts with business needs across the division.
* Ensure compliant IT contracts in close collaboration with Legal, Security, and Data Privacy (DPA), aligning with internal policies and external regulations.
* Collaborate closely with internal stakeholders, including CIO/CTO, Finance, and local entities, to identify, initiate, and execute savings projects, while engaging and supporting local entities in driving compliance and sourcing best practices in a decentralized environment
* Negotiate commercial terms with suppliers, ensuring competitive pricing, favorable conditions, and long-term supplier performance
* Develop, implement, and monitor KPIs for the IT category (Spend development, Savings, Cost Avoidance, Payment Terms, Contract Coverage, Code of Conduct, Project Pipeline, etc.), and ensure visibility of IT spend across the division through robust reporting frameworks
* Establish and continuously improve routines, processes, and governance for IT procurement, including ways of working with Security, Legal, and other key functions, strengthening data-driven decision-making
* Drive cross-divisional collaboration and identify synergies within AAGS, as well as with Group-level IT contracts and other divisions
* Actively contribute to the Indirect Spend team, drive continuous improvement in sourcing activities across the division, and, depending on your experience, take ownership of additional indirect categories (e.g., Professional Services).


**The Skills And Experience You Need**
 We are looking for someone who has:
   

  

* 8+ years of experience in category management, with at least 5 years focused on IT procurement.
* Strong experience working with IT contract compliance, including legal, data privacy, and security considerations.
* Proven track record of delivering measurable results on procurement KPIs, including savings and cost optimization, in a complex international environment.
* Strong experience in managing IT categories, including software licenses, hardware, and other IT-related services.
* Fluency in English (written and spoken).
* Exceptional communication and presentation skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders at all levels.


 You are a proactive, data-driven and structured professional with a strategic mindset and a hands-on approach. You are comfortable navigating complexity and ambiguity, and you enjoy driving change in a global organization.
   

  

 You build strong relationships, challenge the status quo, and know how to balance governance with business needs. Most importantly, you are passionate about creating value through smart sourcing, collaboration, and continuous improvement.
   

  

**What We Offer**
 We’re passionate about providing amazing opportunities and benefits, so you can continue and progress a lifelong career with us – here’s what we have to offer:
   

  

* Learning and career development opportunities, whether it’s online learning, management training or enhancing your skills.
* A competitive salary
* Hybrid model of working
* Stable employment in a friendly international atmosphere


**We review applications regularly, so don't wait.**
 We are building diverse and inclusive teams and encourage applications from all who can envision themselves working with us.
   

  

 To ensure that your personal information is secure, we do not review any applications sent via email or post. If you have any questions about the role or the process, please send an email to Khalil Kabakibi, Talent Acquisition Business Partner, at Khalil.kabakibi@assaabloy.com.
   

  

 Let's together create a safer and more open world!
   

  

 To find out more about us, visit www.assaabloy.com.
   

  

**We are the ASSA ABLOY Group**
 Our people have made us the global leader in access solutions. In return, we open doors for them wherever they go. With nearly 63,000 colleagues in more than 70 different countries, we help billions of people experience a more open world. Our innovations make all sorts of spaces – physical and virtual – safer, more secure, and easier to access.
   

  

 As an employer, we value results – not titles, or backgrounds. We empower our people to build their career around their aspirations and our ambitions – supporting them with regular feedback, training, and development opportunities. Our colleagues think broadly about where they can make the most impact, and we encourage them to grow their role locally, regionally, or even internationally.
   

  

 As we welcome new people on board, it’s important to us to have diverse, inclusive teams, and we value different perspectives and experiences.

title: Domänarkitekt
description:
*Vill du vara med och forma framtidens utvecklarupplevelse? Hos oss får du chansen att bygga hjärtat i vår digitala transformation – plattformar och verktyg som driver innovation, säkerhet och användarvänlighet. Som domänarkitekt agerar du också i rollen som tågarkitekt och blir en central kraft i att skapa en världsledande utvecklingsmiljö där DevSecOps och molntransformation står i fokus. Tillsammans med engagerade kollegor bygger du grunden för nästa generations digitala tjänster. Är du redo att ta ledartröjan och inspirera till innovation och kvalitet?*
**Om jobbet**
 Som domänarkitekt är du en nyckelperson både i linjeorganisationen, där du ansvarar för arkitekturen inom domänen Process & Dev Tool, och i tågledningen, där du främst arbetar på taktisk nivå i nära samarbete med såväl strategisk som operativ nivå. Inom Process & Dev Tools möjliggör vi systemutveckling i världsklass och lägger grunden för effektiva, säkra och skalbara lösningar genom att etablera principer, plattformar, processer och arbetssätt som skapar en modern och hållbar utvecklingsmiljö.
   

  

 Du ansvarar för att definiera och kommunicera en gemensam teknisk och arkitekturell vision för tåget och säkerställer att de system som används och utvecklas passar för sitt syfte och stödjer vår övergripande strategi. Du leder och utvecklar tågets arkitekturella runway, bidrar till planering och prioritering och coachar teamen för att säkerställa att lösningarna håller hög kvalitet och följer den arkitekturella visionen. Du samarbetar med Product Manager, RTE, Business Owners, team, arkitekter och andra intressenter för att säkerställa att våra mål och planer är väl förankrade och att vi håller jämn takt med teknisk skuld. Du är både rådgivare och inspiratör, och driver det tekniska arbetet framåt med moderna metoder och ett DevSecOps-mindset.
   

  

 Dina arbetsuppgifter kommer vara bland annat:
   

  

* Ansvara för att utveckla och kommunicera den tekniska och arkitekturella visionen för tåget
* Säkerställa att arkitekturella behov och möjliggörare (enablers) hanteras i backloggen
* Samverka med tågledningen och relevanta intressenter för att planera, prioritera och koordinera tekniska lösningar
* Bidra till att lösningar håller hög kvalitet och är i linje med verksamhetens mål och strategier
* Coacha utvecklingsteam och främja ett ständigt lärande kring arkitektur och kvalitet
* Tillsammans med resterande tågledning styra tåget för att utveckla rätt lösning utifrån en helhetssyn med utgångspunkt i omvärldens och kundernas framtida behov
* Säkerställa kravställning gentemot leverantör
* Utforma och förvalta "Golden Path" för utveckling och leverans på plattformen, inklusive automatiserade säkerhetskontroller, policy enforcement och audit trails
* Driva kontinuerlig förbättring av plattformens säkerhets- och compliancefunktioner, samt utbilda och stötta utvecklingsteamen i bästa praxis
* Ta en ledarroll i det tekniska arbetet och inspirera till innovation och kvalitet
* Kommunicera och förklara komplexa tekniska frågor på ett pedagogiskt och lättförståeligt sätt för olika målgrupper, samt agera mentor och coach för teamen


 Följande kommer göra dig framgångsrik i denna tjänst:
   

  

* Akademisk utbildning inom datateknik, systemvetenskap, IT eller liknande
* Djup och bred teknisk erfarenhet av systemutveckling och DevSecOps, gärna i större organisationer
* Erfarenhet av att implementera CI/CD och automatiserade pipelines
* Stark förståelse för DevSecOps, automatisering samt security och compliance by design
* Erfarenhet av att implementera och övervaka säkerhets- och compliancekrav i moln- och containerplattformar
* God kunskap om säkerhetsverktyg (t.ex. SAST, DAST, secrets management, IAM) och compliance-ramverk, gärna inom bank och finans
* Kunskap om API-strategier och integrationsmönster
* Förmåga att dokumentera och hålla arkitektur och mönster uppdaterade
* Agilt mindset och erfarenhet av att arbeta enligt SAFe eller liknande ramverk
* Mycket god samarbetsförmåga, lyhördhet och ödmjukhet – du övertygar med väl underbyggda argument och söker alltid den bästa lösningen för helheten
* Förmåga att kommunicera och samarbeta med både tekniska och icke-tekniska intressenter
* Starkt ledarskap och förmåga att motivera, engagera och utveckla andra
* Pedagogisk skicklighet – du kan förklara komplexa tekniska frågor på ett enkelt och tydligt sätt för både tekniska och icke-tekniska intressenter
* Svenska och engelska i tal och skrift


 Meriterande är även om du har:
   

  

* Kunskap inom Azure- och OpenShift/Kubernetes-arkitektur
* Erfarenhet av nätverk, monitorering, logg- och eventhantering
* Kunskap om Key Management, certifikat, autentisering, auktorisering och IAM
* Erfarenhet av IDP-lösningar


 Som person är du nyfiken, drivande och har ett stort engagemang för teknik och utveckling. Du har lätt för att samarbeta och trivs med att arbeta i team, samtidigt som du gärna tar initiativ och leder andra framåt. Du är kommunikativ, pedagogisk och har förmågan att inspirera både tekniska och icke-tekniska kollegor. Du har ett agilt och lösningsorienterat förhållningssätt, är prestigelös och ser alltid till helheten. Din analytiska förmåga och ditt strukturerade arbetssätt gör att du kan hantera komplexa frågor och fatta välgrundade beslut.
   

  

**Kontaktuppgifter:**
 Rekryterande chef:Charlotta.Haldin@lansforsakringar.se
   

  

 Facklig representant Forena:Jessica.loow@lansforsakringar.se
   

  

 Facklig representant Akademikerföreningen:Lisa-Maria.Carensjo@lansforsakringar.se
   

  

*Låter det spännande? Då vill vi veta mer om dig! Välkommen med din ansökan redan idag. Vi arbetar löpande med denna rekrytering och kan komma att tillsätta tjänsten innan sista ansökningsdag.*
 Länsförsäkringar AB är en inkluderande arbetsplats där mod, möjligheter och engagemang står i centrum. Vi erbjuder ett hållbart arbetsliv med flexibla villkor och satsar på att alla medarbetare ska kunna växa och trivas, oavsett bakgrund.
   

  

 Digital & IT är hjärtat i Länsförsäkringsgruppens digitala utveckling. Vi driver den gemensamma digitala transformationen och levererar förstklassiga IT-lösningar, datadrivna insikter och innovativa digitala tjänster som skapar värde för hela verksamheten. Vårt uppdrag är att bygga en robust och framtidssäker IT-miljö – både på plats och i molnet – som möjliggör kundnära och effektiva affärer i hela gruppen.
   

  

 Hos oss möts teknik, affär och innovation. Vi arbetar tvärfunktionellt ute i verksamheten i agila team där utveckling, drift och strategi går hand i hand. Vi ansvarar för allt från IT-styrning, enterprisearkitektur och leverantörshantering till applikationsutveckling, cybersäkerhet och verksamhetsberedskap.

title: Solutions Architect (EMEA North)
description:
At Snowflake, we are powering the era of the agentic enterprise. To usher in this new era, we seek AI-native thinkers across every function who are energized by the opportunity to reinvent how they work. You don’t just use tools; you possess an innate curiosity, treating AI as a high-trust collaborator that is core to how you solve problems and accelerate your impact. We look for low-ego individuals who thrive in dynamic and fast-moving environments and move with an experimental mindset — who rapidly test emerging capabilities to discover simpler, more powerful ways to deliver results. At Snowflake, your role isn't just to execute a function, but to help redefine the future of how work gets done.
   

  

 We are looking for data experts who have a strong background in building data platforms and love to use their skills to supercharge data teams. Our team helps customers on their data journey with Snowflake, which is very fast becoming the central data platform for companies big and small. That means you will work on some of the most challenging data projects you can find, in a post-sales capacity.
   

  

 We are hiring a range of seniority levels, though we do not currently hire people without relevant experience. During the interview process, we will form a view on your current level of expertise and match it to our internal career levels.
   

  

**What we are offering:**
**We help each other to stay on top of the game** 
 and up-to-date with the latest features in the Snowflake Data Cloud. Snowflake had the biggest software IPO of all times and we continue to grow at a rapid pace, which means there is ample opportunity to change the future of data analytics by joining us. Not only will you work with Snowflakes latest and greatest capabilities, but also t
 **he cutting edge technologies of our partners** 
**.**
 Our
 **compensation package** 
 offers a very competitive salary, plus equity on top. We are looking after the work life balance of our employees, which means our annual vacation is at the top of the market. We also have Snowdays - have a look at
 **this LinkedIn post** 
 or other #snowday tagged posts from Snowflakes around the world.
   

  

 For young families we are offering
 **very attractive parental leave** 
 and support with adoption, surrogacy, pregnancy or infant care related questions. Private medical and insurance plans are part of our benefits, in markets where this is a customary employment benefit. Furthermore we provide strong private pension plans and other benefits, tailored to your country of employment.
   

  

**Who we are:**
 The Snowflake Data Cloud was built from the ground up for the modern cloud, which means we can easily scale from empowering small start-ups to the largest companies in the world.
   

  

 It is our mission to
 **mobilize the world's data** 
 , so that businesses can be truly data driven. We think it is important that our platform is easy to use, which allows us to power companies at all stages of the data maturity journey.
 **Here** 
 you can find examples of how we have helped companies in a wide range of industries.
   

  

 We have seen a tremendous amount of success with our platform and continue to grow at a very fast pace, both in Europe and worldwide. That means we are constantly adapting the way we work and input is welcome from everybody on the team, which gives you the chance to make an impact.
   

  

**What we do:**
 We are the service delivery team of Snowflake, which means we help our customers by
 **creating solutions that shape their businesses** 
 . Once our customers have made the decision to implement Snowflake, we accompany them in their journey with us.
   

  

 This journey might start with our migrations team, who help our customers to plan and execute the migration of their data to Snowflake. Or it might begin with a Quick Start consultation, where we simultaneously explain best practices for working with Snowflake and translate them to our customers' specific use case.
   

  

 After a period of time we might return to analyse our customers progress and provide further guidance. We might also look at specific topics such as their security posture.
   

  

 Other customers prefer a more continuous engagement with us. In these cases we stay with them for a year or longer, in order to guide them from the initial setup of the platform all the way to advanced use cases that solve their trickiest challenges.
   

  

**How we work:**
 We
 **provide guidance** 
 to our customers. For most implementation work, we partner with our customers' engineering teams or members of our partner network.
   

  

 While our core expertise is Snowflake, our platform is almost always at the centre of larger data architectures, which we help to design. Examples include architecting for GDPR or integrating with existing data lakes.
   

  

 In addition to helping our customers,
 **we help each other** 
 by answering questions on our internal Slack channels, writing content for our internal knowledge base or organising internal lunch and learn sessions.
   

  

 Currently we work
 **mostly remote** 
 , which has proven to be successful. While we do have region specific practices, we work with customers from across Europe. A consultant from Finland might for example work with a customer in the Netherlands. Internally we work together as a global team.
   

  

**What are we looking for:**
 People who have a strong background in building
 **data platforms** 
 and working on the surrounding data landscape. Some of our colleagues have a stronger background in
 **data warehousing, data engineering** 
 , while others might be more at home on the
 **data science or analytics** 
 side. The data warehouse is where we all come together.
   

  

 Our ideal candidate can translate vague business requirements into technical requirements, find the appropriate solution and communicate both its technical merits and the business impact to stakeholders at different seniority levels. We are constantly adding new features, so explaining new concepts is essential for our success.
   

  

 Since Snowflake is only available in the cloud, having experience of architecting solutions on AWS, Azure or GCP is a big plus. Similarly, having experience with data security, governance and discoverability concepts will be helpful in this role.
   

  

 While
 **SQL** 
 is a language everybody on the team knows very well, Snowflake is fast evolving to allow native integrations with
 **Python, Java** 
 and
 **Scala** 
 & with
 **Snowpark** 
 , if you have experience with
 **Spark** 
 you will definitely be able to put these skills to good use.
   

  

**DataOps** 
 or
 **DevOps** 
 for data teams is getting ever more important. Setting up
 **CI/CD** 
 pipelines with automated tests and rollback capabilities is an activity we regularly engage in.
   

  

**Bonus points to have:**
 We think Data Sharing will play a big role in the next generation of data cloud so if you are familiar with
 **Data Sharing** 
 &
 **Data Marketplace** 
 implementation that would be a big advantage.
   

  

 Snowflake is growing fast, and we’re scaling our team to help enable and accelerate our growth. We are looking for people who share our values, challenge ordinary thinking, and push the pace of innovation while building a future for themselves and Snowflake.
   

  

 How do you want to make your impact?
   

  

 For jobs located in the United States, please visit the job posting on the Snowflake Careers Site for salary and benefits information: careers.snowflake.com

title: Teknisk testare
description:
*Vill du arbeta i ett öppet och engagerat team där du gör skillnad på riktigt?*


*Nu söker vi en teknisk testare till området Kundhandlingar – ett område i utveckling som möjliggör Skandias hantering och utskick av kundkommunikation.*


**Att jobba som teknisk testare**


I rollen som teknisk testare blir du en viktig del av teamet Kundhandlingar, där du arbetar nära utvecklare och andra kompetenser för att säkerställa hög kvalitet genom effektiva teststrategier och automatiserade tester.


Du kommer att arbeta med hela testprocessen – från planering och testdesign till testautomatisering, exekvering och förbättringsarbete.


Rollen innebär stora möjligheter att påverka både kvalitet, arbetssätt och hur vi utvecklar vårt testarbete.  

  



  

I rollen kommer du att:


* Säkerställa effektiva testflöden tillsammans med utvecklare och andra teammedlemmar


* Planera, skapa och exekvera tester med fokus på automatisering, regression och acceptanstester


* Bidra med testkompetens i design och utveckling av lösningar


* Delta i och driva förbättringar av teststrategier och arbetssätt


* Vara en del av Skandias testcommunity och bidra till kunskapsdelning och erfarenhetsutbyte
  

**Om teamet**


Du blir en del av ett agilt team med cirka 10 kollegor bestående av utvecklare, mjukvaruarkitekt, Scrum Master och produktägare.


Teamet präglas av öppenhet, samarbete och en stark vilja att utvecklas tillsammans. Vi arbetar hybrid med moderna lokaler i Stockholm.

  

  

För att lyckas i rollen ser vi att du har erfarenhet av:


* GUI-testning, både manuell och automatiserad
* Testautomatisering, gärna med verktyg som Playwright
* API-testning, exempelvis genom Bruno eller liknande verktyg
* Azure DevOps eller liknande verktyg
* Testdesign, strukturering och underhåll av testfall
Du kommunicerar även obehindrat på svenska i tal och skrift.  

  



Det är meriterande om du har erfarenhet av att läsa och förstå kod.  

  



  

**Som person**


Som person är du strukturerad, självgående och tar ansvar för ditt arbete samtidigt som du trivs med att samarbeta med andra. Du tar initiativ, driver förbättringar inom ditt område och motiveras av att utveckla både lösningar och arbetssätt.


Du delar gärna med dig av din kunskap och är nyfiken på hur ny teknik och AI kan bidra till bättre arbetssätt.  

  



  

**Vad händer nu?**


Vi jobbar med löpande urval så tveka inte att skicka in din ansökan redan idag. I ansökan kommer vi att ställa ett antal urvalsfrågor som ligger till grund för första urvalet.


Läs mer om hela vår rekryteringsprocess här.


Om du har frågor kring rollen, vänligen kontakta rekryterande chef Rina Rosengren Bouius på rina.rosengren.bouius@skandia.se


Om du har frågor kopplat till rekryteringsprocess, vänligen ta kontakt med Nina Fazeli på nina.fazeli@skandia.se.

  

  

Vi kommer att återkoppla till alla som söker oavsett om du går vidare eller inte.  



  

Vi ser fram emot din ansökan!  

  



**Mer om IT**


Vi på Skandia IT är stolta över att vara en kundorienterad enhet som levererar effektiva och säkra lösningar för våra kunder. Med cirka 600 engagerade medarbetare är vi mitt i en spännande transformationsresa, där vi utvecklas tillsammans och har kul på vägen. Framåt tillsammans är vårt motto!


Vi arbetar agilt och har sedan 2017 använt oss av SAFe (Scaled Agile Framework) ramverket, där leverans och kundvärde alltid står i centrum. Våra tågledningar och team, jobbar verksamhetsbaserat med aktiviteter som sprintplanering, daily stand-ups, team-synk, code reviews och retrospektiv.


Var 12:e vecka samlas vi alla i vår ljusa och inspirerande Ljusgård på kontoret i Stockholm för 2 planeringsdagar. Planeringsperioden består av 6 tvåveckorssprintar, där den sista sprinten är en innovationssprint. Här får du chansen att, själv eller tillsammans med ditt team, utveckla och förfina våra system.

Hybridarbete
