 ADP is committed to an inclusive, diverse and equitable workplace, and is further committed to providing equal employment opportunities regardless of any protected characteristic including: race, color, genetic information, creed, national origin, religion, sex, affectional or sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, lawful alien status, ancestry, age, marital status, protected veteran status or disability. Hiring decisions are based upon ADP’s operating needs, and applicant merit including, but not limited to, qualifications, experience, ability, availability, cooperation, and job performance.
   

  

**Ethics at ADP:** 
 ADP has a long, proud history of conducting business with the highest ethical standards and full compliance with all applicable laws. We also expect our people to uphold our values with the highest level of integrity and behave in a manner that fosters an honest and respectful workplace. Click https://jobs.adp.com/life-at-adp/ to learn more about ADP’s culture and our full set of values.

title: Systems Engineer - Mobile Air Traffic Management Systems
description:
*Saab is a place where teamwork makes things fly. Literally. Join us and experience our supportive and friendly workplace.*

  

Your Role


As part of our Mobile Air Traffic Management Systems organisation, you will play a key role in delivering advanced, safety-critical solutions to customers worldwide.  

  

You will be part of a project team together with Project Managers and other technical specialists, where you will help transform complex system concepts into fully operational, real-world deployments. Our systems combine Saab-developed software, proprietary hardware platforms, and integrations with external suppliers - delivered as mobile systems that can be rapidly deployed in demanding environments.  

  

Together with your team, you will contribute throughout the entire system lifecycle, from requirements and system design to integration, verification, deployment, and handover to operational use.  

  

Key responsibilities include:

* Take technical responsibility within customer projects, ensuring system architecture, performance, and quality objectives are achieved
* Work with requirements management, system design, integration planning, and technical coordination
* Drive system-level activities such as configuration, testing, validation, and verification
* Support installation, commissioning, and deployment at customer sites
* Collaborate closely with Project Managers, software developers, hardware engineers, and external suppliers
* Contribute to resolving technical challenges and managing system-level risks and dependencies
* Maintain close dialogue with customers and stakeholders to ensure solutions meet operational needs

  

The role includes travel to customer sites as part of deployment and system acceptance activities.  

  

You will be part of a growing organisation consisting of System Engineers and Project Managers based in Växjö and Gothenburg, with your primary workplace in Växjö. As our organisation continues to grow, you will have the opportunity to contribute to the development of our ways of working and strengthen our delivery capability.


Your Profile


We are looking for a structured and curious System Engineer who enjoys working with complex, integrated systems and wants to be part of delivering solutions that make a real difference.  

  

You are comfortable working in a project-driven environment where requirements evolve, and where collaboration across disciplines is key to success.  

  

To succeed in this role, you have:

* A degree (BSc/MSc) in computer engineering, electrical engineering, software engineering, or a related field
* A few years of experience working with complex technical systems
* Understanding of system development lifecycles, including requirements management, system design, integration, and verification
* Experience working in projects involving both software and hardware components
* Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
* The ability to structure and prioritise work across multiple activities simultaneously
* Good communication skills in Swedish and English, both written and spoken

  

Experience working with safety-critical or regulated systems is considered an advantage, as is experience with deployment, installation, or field activities.  

  

You are a collaborative team player with a positive mindset and a structured approach. You enjoy solving complex problems, collaborating across disciplines, and seeing your work make an impact in real operational environments.  

  

*This position requires that you pass a security vetting based on the current regulations around/of security protection. For positions requiring security clearance additional obligations on citizenship may apply.*

  

What You Will Be Part Of


Explore a wealth of possibilities. Take on challenges, create smart inventions, and grow beyond. This is a place for curious minds, brave pioneers, and everyone in between. Together, we achieve the extraordinary, each bringing our unique perspectives. Your part matters.  

  

Saab is a leading defence and security company with an enduring mission, to help nations keep their people and society safe. Empowered by its 26,100 talented people, Saab constantly pushes the boundaries of technology to create a safer and more sustainable world.  

  

Saab designs, manufactures and maintains advanced systems in aeronautics, weapons, command and control, sensors and underwater systems. Saab is headquartered in Sweden. It has major operations all over the world and is part of the domestic defence capability of several nations. Read more about us here  

  

Saab Air Traffic Management develops and delivers advanced air traffic control tower automation and digital tower systems used by airports and air navigation service providers worldwide. Our solutions are real-time, safety-critical, large-scale systems operating in some of the world’s most demanding operational environments.
company_description: Saab serves the global market with world-leading products, services and solutions from military defense to civil security.

title: Algorithms engineer for EISCAT_3D
description:
EISCAT AB is recruiting an algorithms engineer to develop software forEISCAT\_3D, the world's most advanced scientific radar.
  
The focus of the role is mathematically oriented programming and the implementation of algorithms for the data processing pipeline.
  
EISCAT is currently collaborating with the Swedish Institute of Space Physics (IRF) to develop methods to more accurately measure the orbital parameters of known hard targets, including compensation for wave propagation through the atmosphere. Part of this position at EISCAT is to integrate these algorithms into our current analysis package. It is expected that the work will also include working on the new data analysis pipeline as well as working on development of other parts of the system software.
  
The position is focused on implementing mathematical algorithms and data processing. The candidate should ideally have a good foundation in both mathematics and signal processing, be comfortable working with older codebases and have experience writing high performance code.
  
EISCAT\_3D is expected to be in operation for decades, so we are looking for candidates with a long term view and an interest in radar or radio technology. The team is spread across multiple locations and time zones, so candidates should have good communication skills and be comfortable taking the initiative with their work.
  
Skills and experience
  
  

**Required:**  

Documented experience in mathematics and signal processing
  
Documented experience in programming in two or more of C, Go, Python, and Rust
  
Experience implementing algorithms
  
EU work permit
  
Cat \*B driving license
  
Proficient English language skills, both spoken and written
  
  

**Beneficial:**  

Understanding of radars or experience with amateur radio.
  
Experience with Matlab for work with legacy code used at EISCAT
  
Proficiency working in a Linux environment
  
  

This role is based in Kiruna, Sweden. Remote working may be possible for exceptional candidates.
  
As part of the recruitment process, candidates will undergo security vetting which must be successfully completed prior to employment.
  
EISCAT AB is an international company with English as its working language. All applications must be made in English.

title: Senior Laboratory Engineer
description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Cold Room/Freezers -22degreesF/-6degrees C, Fluctuating Temps hot/cold, Laboratory Setting, Office, Some degree of PPE (Personal Protective Equipment) required (safety glasses, gowning, gloves, lab coat, ear plugs etc.)**Job Description**

**Senior Laboratory Engineer – Allergy Product Verification, R&D Allergy**

**Uppsala, Sweden**


When you’re part of Thermo Fisher Scientific, you’ll do challenging work, and join a team that values performance, quality and innovation. As part of a successful, growing global organization you will be encouraged to perform at your best. With revenues of more than $40 billion and the largest investment in R&D in the industry, we give our people the resources and chances to create significant contributions to the world.


This position is within the ImmunoDiagnostic Division in Uppsala, Sweden. Our division has around 1600 employees worldwide and is market leader within diagnostic tests for allergy and autoimmune diseases. We have our headquarter in Uppsala where we also have our research and development as well as manufacturing for all our allergy diagnostics products.


The Allergy Product Verification group at Thermo Fisher Scientific in Uppsala, is an interdisciplinary team of 11 driven and engaged colleagues that belong to the R&D Allergy department. The group has verification responsibility for most of the company’s allergy diagnostic products. We are now looking to expand and further strengthen our group with a new colleague.

**How will you make an impact?**


As a member of Allergy Product Verification, you will work hands on with verifying that our products meet internal and regulatory requirements while maintaining good quality. You will be involved in planning and performing studies on products within the allergy diagnostics field as well as making sure that our ways of working is efficient and according to best practise.

**What will you do?**

* Plan, perform, evaluate and document verification studies
* Develop our ways of working with our instruments. Maintaining, updating and programming of an internal system.
* Support and collaborate with internal teams

**How will you get here?**

**Education:**

* Applicants should have a Master of Science degree, or equivalent work experience, in Biochemistry, Molecular Biology, Immunology or equivalent.

**Experience:**

* A minimum of 1-3 years of laboratory experience preferred.
* Programming experience is a merit
* Experience with regulatory and quality related activities, ISO and IVD regulations, is a merit

**Knowledge, Skills, Abilities:**

* Self-motivated, independent and strong critical thinking.
* Strong lab skills.
* An interest in system development.
* Individual must work well in a team environment and be adaptable to changes in priorities.
* Strong collaboration skills and cross functional work experience
* Must be organized and detail oriented to be able to prioritize own and other’s work and meet business productivity metrics.
* Good word processing and spreadsheet software skills with excellent oral and written communication skills.
* Proficient verbal and written communication skills in both Swedish and English.


Please apply as soon as possible, the position will be filled as soon as suitable candidate is found!
company_description: At Thermo Fisher Scientific, our professionals develop critical solutions—and build rewarding careers.

title: Human Factors Engineer
description:
**Professional Galaxy** is an IT and technology consulting company that provides highly specialized expertise within IT, software development, SAP, purchasing, electronics and mechanical design. We collaborate with experienced senior experts and deliver strategic value-creating expertise to some of Sweden's most complex and analytically demanding projects. Our focus is always on high quality, professionalism and clear, measurable results.


We are now seeking a **Human Factors Engineer** for one of our clients.

**About the assignment:**


Human Factors Engineer, nivå 3  

  

We are looking for a Human Factors Engineer who focuses on physical & cognitive ergonomics, requirements engineering, testing, and validation, with a solid understanding of product-centric systems engineering.  

  

Human Factors Integration (HFI) is the process of applying human factors within complex engineering programs. A human-centered design requires an integrated approach with other design and engineering activities. It cannot succeed in isolation and requires the active involvement and engagement of systems engineering, design, operations, and safety disciplines within a project team.  

  

Your Role  

* Plan, perform, and coordinate the HFI work within a project team across the entire development lifecycle.
* Translate user needs into technical system requirements and specifications, ensuring human factors are met and validated.
* Apply a systemic approach to understand the product as a whole and how human factors interact with different interfaces (hardware, software, and operator environments).
* Ensure that a human-centered design process and methodology is applied, taking into account usability, usefulness, physical and cognitive ergonomics and user work environment
* Plan, execute, and document tests to verify and validate system requirements.
* Act as the primary point of contact for HF issues with internal and external customers, sub-suppliers, and multidisciplinary project teams.

  

Your Profile  

To thrive in this role, you are a self-motivated, structured professional with strong communication skills and a confident mindset that enables you to adapt to changing project circumstances.  

  

Experience with Figma and Jira is highly desirable. Knowledge of Adobe Illustrator is a merit. Knowledge of the INCOSE framework, systemic safety, or formal ergonomics evaluation methods (such as REBA/RULA/NIOSH or evaluations using mannequins and VR) is a strong asset.  

  

Education: B.Sc. or M.Sc. in Product Design, Industrial Design Engineering, Ergonomics, Cognitive Science or a related field.  

  

**Other Information**  

Assignment Period: 6 Months  

Location: Göteborg
Are you the right person for the assignment, or do you want to recommend a strong candidate? Do not hesitate to contact us.


Please apply directly through our system with:

* Your updated CV in english
* Availability to start the assignment


In the motivation, describe why you are suitable for this assignment - refer to previous consulting assignments, employmxent, education and personal qualities.

**Please note:** We do not accept any applications through mail. All applications have to be sent through the portal to be valid.

**Offer continuously:** Please note that for this role we offer continuously. That means that we sometimes remove the assignments before the deadline. If you are interested, we recommend that you apply immediately.

title: System engineer
description:
*At Tobii, we build technology that enables the next generation of human-machine interaction. Our eye tracking solutions are integrated into products used by some of the world’s leading technology companies across XR and wearable devices.*

*We are now looking for a System Engineer / System Architect to help our customers and internal teams turn complex technical challenges into scalable and robust system solutions. You will collaborate closely with global technology leaders developing next generation XR headsets and wearable products, helping teams make sound technical decisions by creating clarity around interfaces, dependencies, and system behavior.*

 **The Opportunity**  

In this role, you will help bring advanced eye tracking technology from concept to real world products used by millions of people.

  

You will become a key technical partner in complex customer collaborations, helping teams move from uncertainty to clear and actionable solutions. The work spans early product discussions, integration challenges, and technical decision making in projects where performance, usability, and scalability all matter.

  

The role offers a unique combination of deep technology, customer interaction, and cross functional collaboration in an environment where no two projects look the same. It also requires a strong system perspective across software, firmware, electronics, optronics, algorithms, and computer vision.
  

  

**Your mission**

* Support customers in understanding what is required for successful eye tracking integration
* Drive system design and technical alignment across software, hardware, firmware, and algorithms
* Translate customer needs into clear requirements, interfaces, and architectural decisions
* Identify integration risks, technical dependencies, and system trade-offs early
* Coordinate across internal development teams and customer engineering organizations
* Participate hands-on when needed to troubleshoot prototypes, validate assumptions, or analyze system behavior
 **We hope you have**

* Experience working with complex HW/SW product development and system integration
* Background in areas such as embedded software, Linux, electronics, firmware, algorithms, computer vision, sensors/optronics, mechatronics, or complex system integration
* Ability to reason about trade-offs between performance, cost, complexity, robustness, usability, and delivery speed.
* Comfortable reviewing logs, prototypes, specifications, architecture diagrams, and test results to understand system behavior
* Experience working cross-functionally in structured engineering environments
* Strong communication skills and ability to work with both technical teams and customers
 *Any experience from XR, wearable technology, eye tracking or related sensing technologies, and from working internationally is considered a strong plus.*

 **Who are you?**  

You are a curious and pragmatic engineer who enjoys solving complex system challenges together with others. You create clarity in technical environments with many moving parts and build trust through collaboration, communication, and sound engineering judgement.

 **Location**  

This role is based in Stockholm, Sweden, with a 60/40 hybrid work policy.

 **The recruitment process**  

Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. Please submit your application as soon as possible. We look forward to hearing from you!

title: Test Engineer for SW, Electronics & HW
description:
**Description:** **Test Engineer for SW, Electronics & HW**


As a Test Engineer you will be part of our Alarm & Tailgate team. You will plan and execute the verification and validation of the Tailgate functions. You will write test cases/scripts for automation and manual testing, fault trace, analyze logs, and ensure that all administrative work is fulfilled and up to date.


**Deliverables and responsibilities**


* Participate in the process of test system flow by defining test activities, equipment, asset under test and DUT (Device Under Test) preparedness together with requirement owner and suppliers.
* Component-/Domain- and Complete verification in Rig, Boxcar, HIL and on Vehicle. Test methods & test case development in Carweaver and vTestStudio
* Test case writing, planning and coordination of test activities.
* Create Fault reports and participate in the investigation and solution jointly with function or system owners. Review and communicate with suppliers' test reports and results.
* Independently fault trace and take logs and measurement on the component and actuators and signals.
* Experience from automation of scripts scripts and push to CI
* Ensure the work is delivered as per specified acceptance criteria and definition

**Experience and background**


* Swedish B-driver's license a must.
* Experience from Volvo Cars
* Experience within automated verification of system design and software.
* Experiences of following tools preferred: Carweaver, Canalyzer/Canoe, Inca, DSA, Car config edit, Team Center (to release test reports)
* Experience in Test automation tools e.g., VT system and V-Test Studio
* Knowledge and understanding how to read electrical schematics, use of instruments as voltmeters and oscilloscopes.
* Master/Bachelor of Science within electronics, mechatronics or similar.

**Personal qualities**


* Positive mindset
* Able to manage ambiguity
* Strong self-drive
* Communicative and forward leaning.
* Passion for new technologies, software and hardware test and creating great user experiences.

title: Test Engineer – Software, Electronics & Hardware
description:
**About Justera Group**  

Justera Group AB is a leading Swedish IT consulting firm with great experience specializing in building high-performing technology teams and delivering end-to-end IT solutions. Led by dynamic female executives, Justera Group focuses on helping businesses harness the power of digital technologies to continuously evolve in today’s rapidly changing economy. The company has deep expertise in IT consultancy, software development, and recruitment services, providing clients with purpose-driven, well-trained consultants who consistently deliver efficient and effective results. Justera Group manages the entire talent lifecycle, from hiring to ongoing management, ensuring the right fit for each project on time and within budget. With their strong commitment to quality, collaboration, and client success, Justera Group is a trusted partner for technology innovation across Sweden.  

  

**About the Role:**  

We are looking for a Test Engineer with expertise across software, electronics, and hardware domains to join a cross-functional automotive development team focused on body and vehicle functions.


In this role, you will be responsible for planning, executing, and documenting verification and validation activities for embedded vehicle functions. You will work across component, system, and full-vehicle levels, ensuring functionality, reliability, and compliance through structured testing, fault analysis, and close collaboration with engineering stakeholders and suppliers.

 **Key Responsibilities:**

* Plan and execute verification and validation activities for embedded vehicle functions
* Develop and maintain test cases and test scripts for both manual and automated testing
* Define and coordinate test activities, equipment, and device-under-test (DUT) readiness with relevant stakeholders
* Perform component, system, and full-vehicle testing in physical test rigs, hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) environments, and real vehicles
* Develop and execute test methods and test cases using relevant tools and frameworks
* Perform fault tracing, log analysis, and signal/measurement evaluation on components and actuators
* Create, document, and follow up on fault reports, including supporting root cause analysis and corrective actions
* Review and analyze test results and reports from external suppliers and internal teams
* Contribute to test automation efforts and CI integration of test scripts
* Ensure all deliverables meet defined acceptance criteria and quality standards
* Maintain accurate and up-to-date documentation of test activities and results

**Requirements:**

* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Electronics, Mechatronics, Software Engineering, or similar
* Experience in system verification and validation within embedded or automotive environments
* Strong experience in test case design, test execution, and automation scripting
* Ability to perform fault tracing and analyze logs, signals, and measurement data
* Experience working with electrical schematics and measurement tools (multimeter, oscilloscope, etc.)
* Understanding of test system flow and verification processes
* Valid driver’s license (Category B, Sweden)
* Strong communication skills in English  

**Preferred Skills:**
* Experience with automotive test, diagnostic, and flashing tools such as:
* CANalyzer / CANoe
* INCA
* vTestStudio / VT System
* Experience with configuration, calibration, and engineering lifecycle management tools for embedded systems
* Experience with test management and reporting frameworks (e.g., Application Lifecycle Management / PLM systems)
* Experience with CI/CD pipelines and test automation frameworks
* Experience in embedded system testing across hardware and software integration layers

**What We Offer:**

* A dynamic and innovative workplace, driving advancements in cutting-edge robotic technologies
* The opportunity to collaborate with talented cross-functional teams on meaningful and impactful projects
* Competitive compensation and a comprehensive benefits package
* A supportive culture that fosters continuous learning, growth, and professional development
* Flexible hybrid working model for an optimal work-life balance
* 25 days of annual leave to recharge and relax
* An annual wellness allowance of 3,500 SEK to support your health and wellbeing

title: Senior Software Engineer
description:
**Sambla Group is a fintech company with a clear goal – to be your trusted financial partner. We’ve grown into one of the fastest-growing fintech companies - bridging the gap between borrowers and lenders - thanks to the 500+ amazing people who work here.**
**Our success comes from a shared commitment to deliver the best customer journey and empower people to improve their personal finances. Through our well-known brands - Sambla, Advisa, Rahalaitos, Omalaina, Rahoitu and DigiFinans - we help customers get the best possible terms on their loans.**
**Right now we are looking for** 
 Senior Software Engineer who want to be part of this journey, who care about impact, not just output. With a product mindset, you will help shape the next phase of our growth.
   

  

 As Senior Software Engineer at Sambla Group you will work together with a close-knit team of developers on all levels. Everyday work as a developer here revolves around developing our internal systems, working with our partnerbanks API:s and being part of different projects cross-team. A sample of our stack is comprised of the Javascript-stack with focus on Node.js for backend. Having superb skills in our entire stack is not a requirement, but the will to learn is!
   

  

**In This Role, You Will**
* Design and develop backend services powering our new products and ventures
* Actively take part in product discovery and influence what we build.
* Collaborate with frontend, design, and business colleagues to deliver end-to-end value.
* Write clean, reliable, and scalable code with ownership from idea to production.
* Contribute to defining our ways of working and technical patterns as we grow.


**Who You Are**
* 5+ years of backend or fullstack experience with frequent product shipping.
* Strong understanding of modern software engineering principles and architectures.
* Experience with or curiosity for our stack: Node.js, React, TypeScript/JavaScript, Next.js, and AWS (including CDK and CloudFormation)
* Solid experience with APIs, testing, analytics, feature flags, and CI/CD pipelines.
* Bonus: App-first products, React Native or AI-driven workflows.
* Above all: a product mindset, you want to understand why we build things and you see impact as important as delivery.


**Why this role?**
* Shape the foundations: Help set patterns and practices in teams where ways of working are still evolving.
* Build for scale: Work on customer-facing features that reach thousands, while also designing systems that support Sambla’s growth.
* Grow and explore: Join at a stage where team missions and ventures expand, giving you the chance to explore different problem spaces over time.
* Work with purpose: What you build helps everyday people take control of their finances and create lasting impact in their lives.


**What We Offer**
* Business in mind & people at heart – we deliver results together and celebrate our achievements.
* A varied and challenging role in a multinational and highly innovative company, where we value the development of our employees
* We are strong believers in creating a fun workplace where people enjoy coming to work.
* A friendly culture with openness and respect for one another, with the possibility to influence and make a difference on society and people’s everyday lives.


**We might be a little different from most companies. Instead of defining personal values, we’ve built a shared way of working - a rhythm that guides how we collaborate, support our customers, and move forward together.** 
**We call it Drive & Jive - a shared rhythm that creates momentum through direction, collaboration, and human connection.**
 Sambla Group is the new generation of brokers. Our ambition has always been the same - to challenge the banks’ offers so that our customers, regardless of the conditions, should always have the same opportunity to obtain the best possible loan solution. Sambla Group has grown to over 500 employees, established itself in Sweden, Norway, Finland and Denmark with offices in all markets.

title: Senior Software Engineer
description:
**Craft:**
 Technology & Development
   

  

**Job Description:**
 As a Senior Software Engineer in the Gameplay & Monetisation domain, you’ll help build and evolve one of the world’s biggest live mobile game franchises, delivering high-quality, reliable game features and systems that reach millions of players every day. You’ll design, implement, test, optimize, and maintain code while collaborating closely with designers, artists, producers, and fellow engineers in a collaborative, high-impact environment.
   

  

 Soda King’s second largest game, Candy Crush Soda Saga, is loved by millions of players worldwide, matching Swedish fish, rescued Soda gummy bears, and popped soda bottles to win points, defeat obstacles and progress through more than 11,000 levels.
   

  

 We’re an iconic team growing a sweeter future for one of the biggest casual mobile IPs in the world through attracting the world’s best talent. Our ambition is to continue raising the bar for our players when it comes to creativity, product, and technology.
   

  

 Your Role Within the Kingdom
   

  

* Design, architect, implement, and test new and existing game features, with a focus on monetisation systems and features that drive player value and economy engagement.
* Maintain and optimize client-side systems for memory, performance, and device diversity at mobile scale.
* Lead through expertise and collaboration; contribute to technical discovery, evaluate trade-offs, and choose effective methods and techniques to reach outcomes.
* Champion quality through code reviews, well-structured code, and testing practices; proactively identify risks and propose solutions.
* Collaborate in a cross-functional, player-centric team; share knowledge and help teammates grow.


 Skills to Create Thrills
   

  

* Senior individual contributor who takes initiative and often guides the team through their expertise.
* Tackles complex issues requiring in-depth evaluation; goals set in solution or project terms; works with broad autonomy and sound judgment.
* Anticipates needs, drives improvements, and champions impactful projects and initiatives across the product area.


**Experience**
* Proven experience delivering production-quality software for complex, performance-sensitive applications or games.
* Demonstrated ability to design, ship, and iterate on features in a live product environment with cross-functional partners.
* Track record of operating independently on complex problems and influencing solutions across stakeholders.


 Knowledge and skills
   

  

* Strong C++ development skills and comfort working across the mobile client tech stack, or equivalent systems programming experience.
* Solid grasp of performance, memory, and optimization techniques for Android/iOS environments.
* Software engineering fundamentals: clean architecture, testing, debugging, code reviews, and version control workflows.


 Key attributes
   

  

* Player- and quality-focused, with attention to detail and polish.
* Collaborative communicator who shares knowledge and elevates teammates.
* Curious, proactive problem solver who proposes solutions and trade-offs and adapts quickly in a live game setting.


* Experience in professional game development.
* Experience with Android or iOS development, real-time rendering, or performance/memory-constrained applications.


**About King**
 With a mission of
 *Making the World Playful* 
 , King is a leading interactive entertainment company with more than 20 years of history of delivering some of the world’s most iconic games in the mobile gaming industry, including the world-famous Candy Crush franchise, as well as other mobile game hits such as
 *Farm Heroes Sag* 
 a. King games are played by more than 200 million monthly active users. King, part of Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT), has Kingsters in Stockholm, Malmö, London, Barcelona, Berlin, Dublin, San Francisco, New York, Los Angeles and Malta. More information can be found at King.com or by following us on LinkedIn, @lifeatking on Instagram, or @king\_games on X.

title: Software Development Engineer – 2026
description:
**Description**
 Do you want to solve real customer problems through innovative technology? Do you enjoy working on scalable services in a collaborative team environment? Do you want to see your code directly impact millions of customers worldwide?
   

  

 At Amazon, we hire the best minds in technology to innovate and build on behalf of our customers. Customer obsession is part of our company DNA, which has made us one of the world's most beloved brands.
   

  

 Our Software Development Engineers (SDEs) use modern technology to solve complex problems while seeing their work's impact first-hand. The challenges SDEs solve at Amazon are meaningful and influence millions of customers, sellers, and products globally. We seek individuals passionate about creating new products, features, and services while managing ambiguity in an environment where development cycles are measured in weeks, not years.
   

  

 At Amazon, we believe in ownership at every level. As an SDE-I, you'll own the entire lifecycle of your code - from design through deployment and ongoing operations. This ownership mindset, combined with our commitment to operational excellence, ensures we deliver the highest quality solutions for our customers.
   

  

 We're looking for curious minds who think big and want to define tomorrow's technology. At Amazon, you'll grow into the high-impact engineer you know you can be, supported by a culture of learning and mentorship. Every day brings exciting new challenges and opportunities for personal growth.
   

  

 Key job responsibilities
   

  

* Collaborate and communicate effectively with experienced cross-disciplinary Amazonians to design, build, and operate innovative products and services that delight our customers, while participating in technical discussions to drive solutions forward
* Design and develop scalable solutions using cloud-native architectures and microservices in a large distributed computing environment
* Participate in code reviews and contribute to technical documentation
* Build and maintain resilient distributed systems that are scalable, fault-tolerant, and cost-effective
* Leverage and contribute to the development of GenAI and AI-powered tools to enhance development productivity while staying current with emerging technologies
* Write clean, maintainable code following best practices and design patterns
* Work in an agile environment practicing CI/CD principles while participating in operational responsibilities including on-call duties
* Demonstrate operational excellence through monitoring, troubleshooting, and resolving production issues


**Basic Qualifications**
* Education Requirements (must meet one): Currently enrolled in Bachelor's degree or above in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Data Science, Information Systems, or related STEM fields OR Completed Bachelor's or Graduate degree in specified fields
* Expected graduation between October 2025 - September 2026, OR graduated within the last 24 months
* Demonstrated experience with at least one general-purpose programming language such as Java, Python, C++, C#, Go, Rust, or TypeScript
* Demonstrated experience one or more of the following: Data structures implementation, Basic algorithm development, Object-oriented design principles


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Previous technical internship(s) or demonstrated project experience
* Experience with one or more of the following: AI tools for development productivity, Cloud platforms (preferably AWS), Database systems (SQL and NoSQL), Contributing to open-source projects, Version control systems, Debugging and troubleshooting complex systems
* Strong problem-solving and analytical skills
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills
* Demonstrated ability to learn and adapt to new technologies quickly
* Basic understanding of software development lifecycle (SDLC)


 Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates.
   

  

 Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.
   

  

  

**Company** 
 - Amazon Corporate Services Sweden AB - K34
   

  

 Job ID: A3119051

title: AI Enablement Engineer
description:
We are looking for an AI Enablement Engineer. Your mission is to develop and improve our AI capabilities, and make these available across the company.
 



  





 As an AI Enablement Engineer, you will work with the design, implementation, and scaling of AI architecture and solutions. You will follow industry trends connecting the potential of AI with organizational functions.
 



  





 Working closely with senior stakeholders and cross-functional teams, you will ensure that technical solutions align with high impact opportunities and strategic business goal. By translating AI advancements into production-ready skills, agents, frameworks, data and process, you will drive the radical workplace productivity that will build AI into our corporate DNA and future-proof our organization.
 



  





**What you will be doing** 




  




* Define how AI solutions are structured, integrated, and scaled across our company, ensuring they are secure, maintainable, and built on solid data foundations
* Work closely with senior stakeholders to map processes, identify friction points, and prioritise where AI can drive the most meaningful productivity gains
* Build and maintain reusable assets that the whole company benefits from e.g. skill libraries, agent templates, integration patterns, and governance guidelines
* Monitor the evolving AI landscape and bring relevant advancements back into the organisation as practical, production-ready capabilities
* Contribute to building AI literacy and capability, to build AI confidence and embed an AI-first way of working
* Partner with Product Platform and Engineering teams to accelerate AI adoption with within the SDLC
* Ensure AI solutions meet production standards: secure, observable, and maintainable on our GCP stack



  





**What you will do the first 3-6 months in the role** 




  





 Get to know our company and where we are on our AI journey. Begin to influence our AI journey. Establish yourself as a key contributor for AI related matters and get a first few things into live use!
 



  





**Who are you?** 




  





 You are a solution-oriented and self-driven professional with a proactive attitude and a strong sense of ownership. You combine technical curiosity with a deep understanding of how a company functions, enabling you to navigate complex environments and collaborate effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
 



  





 You have the technical depth to implement the "how" using AI. You enjoy a fast-moving setting where priorities can shift, remaining calm and focused under pressure while championing a culture of continuous learning and innovation.
 



  





**Skills and Experience** 




  




* Software engineering background (not just ML theory)
* Hands-on experience with LLM APIs (Anthropic, Copilot, OpenAI etc.)
* Hands-on experience with Google Cloud AI (Vertex AI, Gemini, Bigquery etc)
* Experience from working with SaaS products, ideally from within the Retail software industry
* Good to have - experience from even driven Enterprise Architecture
* Strong communicator - comfortable running demos and writing docs, train the trainer



  





**Getting an idea of our Tech Stack** 




  





 We want to give you an idea of the technical landscape here at Extenda Retail. As a heavy adopter of cloud, we have solutions in Google Cloud Platform (Cloud Run, Pub/Sub, Vertex AI, Gemini). We embrace a reactive microservice architecture and utilize Kafka in Confluent Cloud. In our tech stack, you will also find GitHub Actions, Kubernetes, Jira, and Confluence. Our AI strategy is anchored in leveraging these tools to build a high-velocity, agent-first workforce.

title: SW Engineer - C++ on Embedded Linux
description:
Vi har nu ett nytt behov och söker därför en konsult och kollega med nedan kvalifikationer. Tjänsten är en del av vår konsultverksamhet vilket innebär att du blir anställd av oss och arbetar hos kund alt. interna projekt och åtaganden.
   

  

**Din profil (Skall krav)**
* Har mycket god erfarenhet av C++ och embedded Linux
* Är trygg i att arbeta i komplexa systemmiljöer
* Kan ta ansvar, driva arbete självständigt och identifiera risker
* Trivs i samarbete med andra team och har ett helhetsperspektiv


**Sista ansökningdatum:** 
 14-06-2026
   

  

**Vi erbjuder dig följande, Valet är ditt!**
 🚀 Spännande uppdrag hos våra många kunder. Vi fokuserar på långsiktiga uppdrag, vilket ger dig möjlighet att skapa värde för kunden samtidigt som du utvecklas själv.
   

  

 🌟 En trygg månadslön – tillsammans bestämmer vi en grundlön baserad på dina uppdrag.
   

  

 💼 Tjänstepension och stora möjligheter till löneväxling.
   

  

 🌴 Semester - Önskar du mer än 30 dagar semester, kanske 60 dagar?
   

  

 🏥 Privat sjukvårdsförsäkring, olycksfallsförsäkring och livförsäkring för ökad trygghet.
   

  

 📚 Kompetensutveckling – du får själv sätta din budget och välja vilka utbildningar du vill delta i.
   

  

 🕑 Livskvalitet - Har du kanske svårt att få ihop pusslet i vardagen och önskar arbeta lite mindre och ändå kunna tjäna lika mycket om inte mer?
   

  

* Vi hanterar ansökningar löpande, med hänsyn till höga arbetstrycket är det extra viktigt med en kort motivering för tjänsten som förklarar hur man möter kraven.

title: Acne Studios System Developer
description:
Acne Studios is a progressive luxury house and through collaboration and curiosity, we set high aspirations and strive for excellence. We value everyone's contribution and embrace feedback to develop ourselves and others. We aim to minimize our environmental impact across all our operations and to address societal challenges in the way we conduct our business.
   

  

 As a System Developer in our Stockholm headquarters, you'll join a skilled development team working at the intersection of fashion, retail, and technology. This role is ideal for someone with 1-2 years of experience in Dynamics 365 F&O and Azure integrations who is ready to take the next step and deepen their expertise in a dynamic, global environment.
   

  

 You'll collaborate closely with senior developers, testers, IT Business Partners, and teams across the company. Together, you'll design, build, and maintain solutions that support our global operations - from purchasing and logistics to retail and finance. Our tech stack includes Dynamics 365 F&O, Azure integration services, and Azure DevOps.
   

  

 CULTURE OF COLLABORATION
   

  

 We firmly believe that when you feel supported, included, and trusted, it enables you to enjoy your work and thrive in it. You will be a part of a team of colleagues with diverse roles and backgrounds, each with their unique skills and experiences.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
 As System Developer your main contribution will be to build and maintain solutions that support our global operations. You will report to the Head of System Development & Business Relations. You will play a vital role in the development team where you will:
   

  

* Develop, test, deploy, and maintain software solutions and integrations that align with our architectural and coding standards
* Grow your craft with coaching from experienced senior developers
* Contribute to solution design and take an active role in technical discussions
* Write clean, structured, and well‑documented code
* Participate in code reviews and offer constructive, thoughtful feedback
* Troubleshoot and resolve defects, performance challenges, and technical debt
* Partner with IT Business Partners to assess needs and estimate development efforts
* Provide third‑line support and help diagnose production issues
* Collaborate closely with QA to refine testing practices and shape effective test strategies
* Highlight risks, gaps, and potential business impact throughout development
* Continuously seek ways to improve our tools, processes, and systems
* Share knowledge generously and contribute to a collaborative, learning‑focused culture


**Who You Are**
 You have high aspirations and focus on details that create substantial impact. You enjoy working as a team and you embrace feedback to develop yourself and others. You are curious and thrive in a dynamic environment. Cooperation, respect, and clear communication are important, along with your ambition to learn and grow. If these qualities speak to you, we would love to have you on our team.
   

  

**Specific Personal Qualities For This Role**
* You enjoy solving problems and understanding how systems support business processes
* You're driven by improving how things work and creating clarity in complexity
* You feel comfortable asking questions, sharing ideas, and collaborating closely with others
* You're eager to grow your skills - and you value a workplace where clarity, simplicity, and quality truly matter


**Specific Experience And Knowledge For This Role**
* 1-2 years of hands‑on experience with Dynamics 365 F&O development (X++)
* Experience with Azure integrations is a strong plus
* A solid understanding of software development principles and coding standards
* Experience working in - or supporting - processes within retail is an advantage
* A structured, solution‑oriented approach to work
* Curiosity and an interest in AI, automation, and innovation
* A collaborative mindset and a genuine interest in learning and contributing
* Fluency in English


 What matters most to us is not whether you master all the listed knowledge and skills. We are dedicated to recognising the potential in each individual. We embrace diversity of people as well as ideas and viewpoints and encourage everyone's individual expression of their own identity.
